title: SFS Message
description: A single message entry in a SessionFS messages.jsonl file. Supports tree-structured conversations (via parent_msg_id) and flat sequences.

msg_id.description: Unique identifier for this message. UUID v4 or v7.

parent_msg_id.description: ID of the parent message for tree-structured conversations. Null for root messages or flat sequences.

role.description: The role of the message author. 'developer' is for tool-injected context (e.g., Codex permissions). 'tool' is for tool result messages. 'system' is for system-level events.

content.description: Ordered list of content blocks. A message may contain multiple blocks (e.g., text + tool calls).

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this message was created.

turn_id.description: Groups messages into conversational turns. All messages in a turn share the same turn_id. Optional for tools that don't have explicit turn boundaries.

model.description: Model identifier used to generate this message (assistant messages only).

stop_reason.description: Why the model stopped generating. Null for non-assistant messages or streaming chunks.

usage.description: Token usage for this message. Typically present on assistant messages.

is_sidechain.description: True if this message belongs to a sub-agent sidechain rather than the main conversation.

is_meta.description: True for system-injected messages (e.g., session resumption context). Should be preserved but may be filtered in display.

agent_id.description: Identifier of the sub-agent that produced this message. Null for main conversation messages.

metadata.description: Extensible key-value pairs for source-specific data that doesn't fit the canonical schema. Converters should preserve unknown fields here.

content_block.description: A single content block within a message. Discriminated by the 'type' field.

text.description: The thinking/reasoning content. Empty string if redacted.

signature.description: Cryptographic signature from the API proving authenticity (Anthropic). Preserve as-is.

redacted.description: True if the thinking content was encrypted or unavailable from the source tool. The text field will be empty.

summary.description: A plaintext summary of the thinking, if available (e.g., Codex reasoning summaries).

tool_use_id.description: Unique identifier for this tool invocation. Used to match with the corresponding tool_result.

input.description: The tool input. Structure varies by tool. For shell commands, may be {"command": [...]} or {"command": "..."}.

tool_use_id.description: ID of the tool_use block this result corresponds to.

content.description: The tool output as text. Large outputs may be truncated with a note.

is_error.description: True if the tool execution failed.

exit_code.description: Process exit code for shell/command tool results.

type.description: 'url' for remote images, 'base64' for inline data, 'path' for workspace-relative file paths.

data.description: The URL, base64 data, or relative file path.

text.description: Plaintext summary of compacted/summarized conversation history.

summarized_through_msg_id.description: The msg_id of the last message included in this summary.

unknown_block.description: Catch-all for content block types not yet in the spec. Preserves source data for forward compatibility.

token_usage.description: Token usage statistics for a message.

cache_read_tokens.description: Number of tokens read from cache.

cache_write_tokens.description: Number of tokens written to cache.
